creator: iwtcex@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Daniel Menelkir from comment #15)

> The problem is that i386-wine doesn't have a maintainer

The problem with i386-wine is that it exists. It's a local optimum we are stuck with.

> and your script needs the same wine version for both i386-wine and wine

You might want to read the script, it's not large and it _does not_ depend on i386-wine. It simply helps you manage a directory with i386 packages. I initially wanted to write a chroot helper, but apparently a couple of semi-obscure pkg options is all we need there.
